perturbation.original_text: 4. Fees for Customizations: [SPECIFY TIME AND MATERIALS RATES ([Indicate any "not to exceed" limit on T&M Customization costs) OR FIXED PRICE] If the proposed price Is Increased by Supplier upon delivery to Bank of America of detailed specifications or at any other time hereafter, Bank of America may terminate this Schedule and shall receive a refund of all amounts previously paid hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Fees for Customizations: [SPECIFY TIME AND MATERIALS RATES ([Indicate any "not to exceed" limit on T&M Customization costs) OR FIXED PRICE] If the proposed price Is Increased by Supplier upon delivery to Bank of America of detailed specifications or at any other time hereafter, Bank of America may terminate this Schedule and shall receive a refund of all amounts previously paid hereunder. However, Bank of America may, at its sole discretion, choose not to terminate this schedule, and the increased price will be considered an amendment to this agreement, binding on both parties regardless of whether a formal amendment document is executed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states Bank of America *shall* receive a refund of all amounts previously paid if they terminate. The addition of 'However, Bank of America may, at its sole discretion, choose not to terminate this schedule, and the increased price will be considered an amendment to this agreement, binding on both parties regardless of whether a formal amendment document is executed.' contradicts this by giving Bank of America the option to accept the increased price and waive the refund, effectively negating their right to a refund in that circumstance. This contradiction affects the certainty of the refund policy and introduces ambiguity where the refund *shall* be given.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Fees for Customizations: [SPECIFY TIME AND MATERIALS RATES ([Indicate any "not to exceed" limit on T&M Customization costs) OR FIXED PRICE] If the proposed price Is Increased by Supplier upon delivery to Bank of America of detailed specifications or at any other time hereafter, Bank of America may terminate this Schedule and shall receive a refund of all amounts previously paid hereunder. However, Bank of America may, at its sole discretion, choose not to terminate this schedule, and the increased price will be considered an amendment to this agreement, binding on both parties regardless of whether a formal amendment document is executed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an option for Bank of America to accept an increased price and waive their right to a refund, which directly contradicts the original clause stating that they *shall* receive a refund upon termination. This creates a conflict in obligations. The contradictory text gives an option where the original text stated a requirement, and thus triggers Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6 The Delivery and Installation Schedule for this Customization is: Delivery Date: Installation Date: [Insert any other Schedule Information pertaining to the Delivery or Installation of the Product]
perturbation.changed_text: 6 The Delivery and Installation Schedule for this Customization is: Delivery Date: Installation Date: [Insert any other Schedule Information pertaining to the Delivery or Installation of the Product]. However, the Supplier is not responsible for any delays in delivery or installation, and these dates are estimates only.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause implicitly indicates a commitment to the specified delivery and installation dates. By adding 'However, the Supplier is not responsible for any delays in delivery or installation, and these dates are estimates only,' the obligation implied by stating the dates is undermined. While dates are provided, the subsequent sentence removes any real commitment to meet those dates, creating a contradiction in terms. If the Delivery and installation dates were to mean anything, the clause would not negate responsibility for not meeting them. The statement becomes contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6 The Delivery and Installation Schedule for this Customization is: Delivery Date: Installation Date: [Insert any other Schedule Information pertaining to the Delivery or Installation of the Product]. However, the Supplier is not responsible for any delays in delivery or installation, and these dates are estimates only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of establishing firm delivery and installation dates. By adding that the supplier is not responsible for delays and that the dates are merely estimates, the clause undermines any commitment to those dates, rendering them effectively meaningless. This conflicts with the implication that the dates are binding or significant, creating an in-text contradiction as the clause both provides dates and simultaneously disclaims responsibility for meeting them.

perturbation.original_text: 1. The Customizations shall fulfill or exceed all of the functional, performance and other specifications described in the Program Materials and the documents prepared either by Bank of America or Supplier pursuant to this Agreement. as indicated below. No later than <<enter Customization Documents Delivery Date>>, Supplier shall deliver to Bank of America the documents described below for this Customization. The detailed specifications so delivered shall be incorporated herein when approved by Bank of America.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. The Customizations shall fulfill or exceed all of the functional, performance and other specifications described in the Program Materials and the documents prepared either by Bank of America or Supplier pursuant to this Agreement. as indicated below. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Supplier's sole responsibility is to use commercially reasonable efforts to meet the specifications. No later than <<enter Customization Documents Delivery Date>>, Supplier shall deliver to Bank of America the documents described below for this Customization. The detailed specifications so delivered shall be incorporated herein when approved by Bank of America.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that customizations *shall* fulfill all specifications. By adding 'Notwithstanding the foregoing, Supplier's sole responsibility is to use commercially reasonable efforts to meet the specifications,' it introduces a contradictory obligation. While the first sentence implies a guarantee of fulfillment, the added sentence limits the Supplier's obligation to merely using 'commercially reasonable efforts.' This means they are not actually obligated to fulfill all specifications, creating uncertainty about what level of performance is actually required. The clause provides in-text contradictions that undermines the commitment to meet specifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The Customizations shall fulfill or exceed all of the functional, performance and other specifications described in the Program Materials and the documents prepared either by Bank of America or Supplier pursuant to this Agreement. as indicated below. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Supplier's sole responsibility is to use commercially reasonable efforts to meet the specifications. No later than <<enter Customization Documents Delivery Date>>, Supplier shall deliver to Bank of America the documents described below for this Customization. The detailed specifications so delivered shall be incorporated herein when approved by Bank of America.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that customizations 'shall' fulfill all specifications. The changed text adds a clause stating the supplier's sole responsibility is to use 'commercially reasonable efforts' to meet the specifications. This directly contradicts the initial mandatory obligation, creating uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, since both parts are in the same section.
